channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court rejects GOP-backed Montana case based on controversial election law theory
item.description: The Supreme Court has turned back an election law case out of Montana that relied on a controversial legal theory with the potential to change the way elections are run across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is moving forward this week, with Senate hearings scheduled for several of his Cabinet picks. Many nominees have met with senators individually.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump brings back Diet Coke button to White House Oval Office
item.description: President Trump's famous "Diet Coke Button" was spotted on the Resolute Desk in the Oval Office at the White House again on Monday after the inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump grants sweeping pardon of Jan. 6 defendants, including rioters who violently attacked police
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has pardoned, commuted the prison sentences or vowed to dismiss the cases of all of the 1,500-plus people charged with crimes in the Jan. 6, 2021, U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump is withdrawing from the Paris Agreement (again), reversing U.S. climate policy
item.description: President Donald Trump issued a barrage of orders Monday targeting Biden-era initiatives on global warming. The decisions come on the heels of the hottest year ever recorded.
item.dc:creator: Nate Perez and Rachel Waldholz, MPR News

item.title: Trump moves to suspend clearances of ex-intel officials who signed letter on Hunter Biden laptop
item.description: President Donald Trump says his administration will move to suspend the security clearances of the more than four dozen former intelligence officials who signed a 2020 letter saying that the Hunter Biden laptop saga bore the hallmarks of a “Russian information operation.”.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump orders end of humanitarian parole for migrants of four countries
item.description: Under the Biden administration, migrants from embattled countries could apply for entry due for humanitarian reasons, without having to attempt to cross into the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Trump temporarily halts leasing and permitting for wind energy projects
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday temporarily halting offshore wind lease sales in federal waters and pausing the issuance of approvals, permits and loans for both onshore and offshore wind projects.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden pardons his siblings and their spouses on his way out of the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joe Biden pardoned his siblings and their spouses on his way out of the White House, saying Monday that his family had been “subjected to unrelenting attacks and threats, motivated solely by a desire to hurt me — the worst kind of partisan politics.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, COLLEEN LONG and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate panel advances Noem nomination for Homeland Security secretary
item.description: A Senate committee cleared President Trump's nominee to lead the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) with a bipartisan vote, paving the way for a Senate floor vote. The Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee advanced South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R) by a 13-2 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order directing US withdrawal from the Paris climate agreement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday directing the United States to again withdraw from the landmark Paris climate agreement, dealing a blow to worldwide efforts to combat global warming and once again distancing the U.S. from its closest allies.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY and SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive orders on federal workforce, free speech
item.description: President Trump signed multiple executive orders during his inauguration parade at Capital One Arena on Monday evening, throwing pens to supporters after a signing ceremony. The signings at a small desk set up on stage in the Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inside the intimate inauguration: Close-up encounters between political rivals, some awkward
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A president’s inaugural address is typically a choreographed spectacle. A makeshift grandstand is erected next to the Capitol, hundreds of thousands of people line the National Mall and the images and words of the day endure for generations. This time was different.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, STEPHEN GROVES, JULIA NIKHINSON, and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marco Rubio unanimously confirmed as secretary of State, becoming Trump's first Cabinet official
item.description: Rubio’s confirmation vote passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, 99-0. Rubio also was able to cast a vote for himself.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Democrats congratulate, caution Trump as he starts 2nd term
item.description: Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation -- all Democrats -- congratulated and cautioned President Donald Trump as he began his second term Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden commutes sentence for Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier, convicted in killing of FBI agents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier will return home nearly half a century after he was imprisoned for the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER, JOHN HANNA and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At Trump's second inaugural, a changed Washington and a full embrace from Republicans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump took the oath of office on Monday inside a tightly packed Capitol Rotunda, where he was surrounded by a very different Washington than he was eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk already has a White House email address
item.description: "It seems to confirm he's going to have an EEOB office," the source said, using the acronym for Eisenhower Executive Office Building.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's communication led to second presidency: Sam Donaldson
item.description: He suggested some of what Trump said highlighted the starkest departure from a past administration since Herbert Hoover and Franklin Roosevelt.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to make historic move towards revoking birthright citizenship
item.description: Trump wants to reinterpret the phrasing of the 14th Amendment to mean that the federal government would not recognize automatic birthright citizenship for children born in the U.S. to parents without legal status.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Inauguration Day Latest: Trump issues pardons for Jan. 6 rioters and signs more executive actions
item.description: Donald Trump has been sworn in as the 47th president of the United States, taking charge as Republicans claim unified control of Washington and set out to reshape the country’s institutions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, PIX 11

item.title: Trump sets out to erase Biden's legacy with pardons and orders immediately after taking office
item.description: Donald Trump has begun erasing Joe Biden’s legacy after taking office as the nation’s 47th president.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Chris Megerian And Michelle Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden pardons Virginia House Speaker Don Scott, Black nationalist Marcus Garvey
item.description: President Joe Biden pardons five people and commutes the sentence of two others who "made significant contributions to improving their communities.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Lawler: Trump has right to revoke congestion pricing approval
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Congestion pricing began in New York City earlier this month. Under congestion pricing, drivers are charged a $9 toll during peak hours to enter Manhattan’s central business district at and below 60th Street. U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about the special election for Vargas' Board of Supervisors seat
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors on Tuesday finalized plans to fill the seat formerly held by Nora Vargas before her abrupt exit from elected office through a special election.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Florida's Rubio and Bondi face confirmation hearings
item.description: Former state attorney general and Tampa native Pam Bondi and Senator Marco Rubio are set to appear on Capitol Hill for confirmation hearings, Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Few US adults confident Justice Department and FBI will act fairly under Trump, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: As President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi, prepares for questioning from senators on Wednesday, only about 2 in 10 U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: County Board of Supervisors votes to hold special election to fill District 1 seat
item.description: In a swift and unanimous vote, the County Board of Supervisors has agreed to a special election to fill the vacancy left behind by Nora Vargas.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Vara, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada elections leader urges real-time efficiency over changing deadlines
item.description: The influx of mail-in ballots collected on Election Day led to delays in tabulation across Nevada, not the number of ballots received in the days after, Democratic Nevada Secretary of State Cisco Aguilar said ahead of the legislative session.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
